items.q: What does 'piano' (p) mean as a dynamic marking?
items.why: Students see 'piano' and think of the instrument. As dynamic, p = soft (from Italian). The instrument is actually a shortened 'pianoforte' (soft-loud) because it could play at varying dynamics, unlike the harpsichord.

items.q: What does the time signature 6/8 indicate?
items.correct: Two beats per bar, each beat divided into three (compound duple).
items.misconception: Six beats per bar, all equal in stress.
items.why: Students count 6/8 as six equal beats. In practice it's felt as TWO dotted-crotchet beats subdivided into three quavers each - compound duple, not simple sextuple.

items.correct: Three sections: A, contrasting B, and a return to A (ABA).
items.why: Students assume 'three sections' means three different ones. Ternary has only TWO distinct ideas, but A returns - it's the return that makes it ternary, not the section count alone.

items.q: Treble lines from bottom up spell.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: EGBDF. “FACE” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's FACE. “BAGS” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many lines on a stave?
items.why: The real figure is 5. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
